key_issues: All 11 iteration-001 direct instructions and every detail requirement are resolved correctly: S5 owns the initialize handler + server-side negotiation; S6 split into S6a (transports) / S6b (high-level server); S7 split into S7a (client-driven) / S7b (server session-state); S5 interop is binary Racket-only with the first cross-SDK leg gated in S6a; S1 maps core/types/errors.ts and tests the -32042/-32004 decode direction; S7b owns the server-raised -32042; the logging/setLevel S5-stub->S7b-filter contract is explicit; S2 enumerates a minimum JSON-Schema keyword set with per-keyword TS-baseline tests; S9 declares a curated 7-example subset including the inMemoryEventStore resumption example; M5e orphaned-until-S6a and related-request-id first-load-bearing-in-M8 are annotated; S8's two concrete deps (S4 ctx + S6a M8 seam, not S7) are stated.

Worker's two new technical claims verified against the TS checkout: the resumption EventStore is a real injectable port (streamableHttp.ts:27 storeEvent/replayEventsAfter, eventStore? injection, InMemoryEventStore implements it) so the append-event/replay-after seam is faithful; related-request-id is the send-option that routes server-initiated messages onto the correct SSE stream (streamableHttp.ts:960-968).
Milestone granularity restored to credible ~1-week single-agent units; dependency graph correct and never inverts layering; three parallel edges (S6a||S6b, S7a||S7b, S8||S7) are sound.
MINOR (non-blocking): S7a should state its sampling/elicitation tests originate the server request via a test stub / S6b server so it reads as genuinely client-only.

MINOR (non-blocking): keep streamableHttp.ts parity at 'partial' after S6a; flip to 'done' at S7 when server-initiated flows over HTTP (the load-bearing use of related-request-id) are actually exercised.
MINOR (non-blocking): S8 lists cross-app access as a deliverable + parity row but has no acceptance gate exercising it; add a gate or annotate as parity-only.
MINOR (non-blocking): S6a's M8 deliverable should explicitly state both session-bearing and sessionless (stateless) operation to substrate the S9 stateless-HTTP example (#3).
